Thanks for reviewing our contract, Rafael. I think the terms are fair. Is there anything you would like to discuss?
Mostly, yes. But I have one question about the scope. If we needed extra work later, how would we handle it?
Good question. Any new work needs a written amendment that both parties sign. And we will comply with the 90-day deadline.
My main concern is the liability. If a serious data breach happened, the cap would be too low. If I were you, I would offer a higher limit.
I understand. If you needed a higher limit for security incidents, we could add a separate insurance clause for that risk.
That works for me. Send the new clause to my legal team, and if everything looks good, we would sign off by the end of the month.
Overall, the terms of the contract look fair.
The vendor must comply with the ninety-day deadline.
If I were you, I would ask for a higher liability limit.
We only sign off when both parties agree on the terms.

Hi team, this is Rafael with a quick summary of the new vendor contract, so legal can review it before we sign off. Overall, the terms look fair, but I want to flag three clauses. First, the scope. The scope is clear: the vendor will deliver the cloud migration and three months of support. If the scope changed later, we would need a written amendment, so nobody adds work for free. Second, the deadline and the penalty. The vendor must comply with a ninety-day deadline. If the vendor missed that deadline, we would apply a penalty of two percent per week. I think that is fair, but I want legal to confirm it. Third, liability. Right now, the liability clause is capped at the value of the contract. If a serious data breach happened, our exposure would be limited to that amount. I am not comfortable with that cap, so if I were you, I would ask for a higher limit on security incidents. To sum up: the terms are good, but before we sign off, please check the scope amendment, the penalty, and the liability cap. Thanks, and send me your comments in the thread.

Hi Rafael, this is Sofia from the vendor side, with a short follow-up after our call. Thank you for your clear questions today. I want to confirm a few terms in writing. On the scope, you are right: the contract covers the cloud migration and three months of support, and any new work needs a written amendment that both parties sign. On the deadline, we are confident we can comply with the ninety-day window. We accept the penalty clause for late delivery, but we would like to cap it at ten percent of the total fee. On liability, I understand your concern about a data breach. If you needed a higher limit for security incidents, we could offer a separate insurance clause for that specific risk. If we agreed on those three points, I think we would have a contract that protects both sides. Please share this with your legal team, and if everything looks good, we could sign off by the end of the month. Talk soon, and thank you again.

First, read the whole contract and check that the scope matches what you actually need. Next, find the deadline and the penalty, and ask what happens if the vendor delivers late. Then, look at the liability clause and check the cap, because that is your protection in a data breach. After that, list every clause you want to change, and propose clear, polite amendments. Finally, send your comments to legal, and only sign off when both parties agree on the terms.
